positive.content: I just feel like no one I work with really likes me or cares about me
[Restatement or Paraphrasing] You don't think people like you or care about you at work? What specifically have they done to make you think this?

negative.content: I don't want to answer questions — I'm not going to explain myself to you.
[Strategy: Reflection of feelings] It seems like you're feeling guarded and maybe tired of talking about it right now.

messages.content: [Question] I'm sorry to hear this. Is there something specifically bothering you?
I just feel like no one I work with really likes me or cares about me
[Restatement or Paraphrasing] You don't think people like you or care about you at work? What specifically have they done to make you think this?

[Affirmation and Reassurance] I can understand that might make you feel uncomfortable.

negative.content: I'm not answering that. Why do you need to know? Can't you just tell me what to do?
[Strategy: Self-disclosure] I get wanting a quick fix—I once snapped when someone probed too much and shut down. If it's okay, I can share a few things that helped me when I felt sidelined.

messages.content: [Restatement or Paraphrasing] You don't think people like you or care about you at work? What specifically have they done to make you think this?
I just feel like no one talks to me and if I try and talk to them they are very dismissive

positive.content: I feel like I go out of my way to help people and I remember their birthdays and I try and be nice to them but no one ever does the same for me

[Self-disclosure] I have been in many work situations and even social situations where I did not feel as if I fit in, I'm rather an unusual person. I had to learn to not focus so much on BEING accepted. I had to learn what God think of me (he loves me) and try to be kind to all I meet, other centered. That is, I also had to make sure I wasn't being rude or mean. I can understand how hurtful that is. Our society isn't as kind as it once was. I find very few people appreciate kindness. However, I can't get caught up in that, it's the way people are. It isn't a reflection of myself. I think it is the hardest thing to be more concerned with others than we are with ourselves, it can be lonely.

negative.content: No, I don't want to talk about specifics. It's pointless — they always act the same, why bother explaining?
[Strategy: Question] I hear you. If you don't want specifics, can you tell me one thing that would make work less draining for you right now?

Because if we say yes because we CAN'T say no, then we feel resentful. So If I can freely say no to you, then i am free to say yes, if that makes sense. positive.content: Thank you! You have been very helpful. I think that will be my goal! Start learning to say no and don’t feel guilty about it. I think I am going to lower my expectations from other people and just start treating people as they treat me!

[Providing Suggestions] If I always say yes, because I can't say no, I feel obligated and resentful, when really, it's me with the problem, I had a hard time saying no because I didn't want to hurt people. Now, I  do the right thing in my heart, if I want to help, I help, if I want to say no, I say no even if it is hard, I don't care if they get upset. I can say no, that's true freedom. I don't advise treating people the way they treat you though. I would remain a kind person. Be the person you are no matter what type of person they are. You have to stand before God one day. If they are ugly, that's on them. But you want to be a kind person. That's the right thing, and you CAN be kind and say no. Those two are fine together! :D I suggest a book called "Bounderies" one of the authors is Townsend -- great book.
